user_intent_preservation.original_goal: Implement 3-phase PWM motor control on TC4D7 using eGTM/ATOM with unified IfxEgtm_Pwm.
user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: Generate a 3-phase complementary, center-aligned PWM inverter on KIT_A3G_TC4D7_LITE using ATOM0 Cluster 0 at 20 kHz with 1 µs rising/falling dead-time via DTM, initial duties U/V/W = 25/50/75%, atomic +10% duty steps every 500 ms, synchronized start and synchronized updates enabled, with a period-event ISR toggling LED P03.9. Enable eGTM GCLK, FXCLK0 for ATOM, and CMU CLK0 for DTM.
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Use the unified high-level IfxEgtm_Pwm driver to configure ATOM0 Cluster0 channels as one base + contiguous sync channels with complementary outputs via DTM and center alignment. Enable eGTM clocks (GCLK, FXCLK0, CMU.CLK0) and use the driver's synchronized start/update to ensure atomic multi-channel duty changes. Route TOUT pins per IfxEgtm_PinMap via the driver config, set HS active-high and LS active-low with push-pull and cmosAutomotiveSpeed1 pad driver. Configure the period-event interrupt in the IfxEgtm_Pwm config to toggle LED P03.9 in its ISR.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: Unified high-level PWM driver supports multi-channel synchronized center-aligned PWM with complementary outputs and DTM-based dead-time on eGTM/ATOM. It natively provides syncStart/syncUpdate and atomic multi-channel duty updates required by the user.

runtime_function.name: updateEgtmAtom3phInvDuty
runtime_function.purpose: Atomically step 3-phase complementary PWM duties by +10% every 500 ms using synchronized (shadow) updates; immediate update variant available for on-the-fly changes.

driver_changes: IfxScuWdt -> IfxWtu (watchdog handling in startup if needed)
migration_notes.context_preserved: The unified IfxEgtm_Pwm driver on TC4xx configures ATOM0 Cluster0 with complementary center-aligned PWM, DTM-based 1 µs dead-time, synchronized start/update, specified TOUT pin routing, and a period-event ISR to toggle LED P03.9, meeting the exact frequency, duty, polarity, pad, and atomic update timing requirements.

PWM.rationale: Driver locked by refiner node (user explicit or engine-scored)
